LAW AND MOTION, 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT WINCO WINDOW COMPANY INC.'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT OF PROGRESS GLASS COMPANY, INC. IS OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AND DECEIT AND THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM AS THE ASSIGNEE OF NIBBI AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR A PASS THROUGH CLAIM AS AN ASSIGNED OF NIBBI. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS ALLEGED WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY AND THE PLEADING REQUIREMENTS ARE RELAXED WHERE THE CROSS-DEFENDANT OUGHT TO POSSESS FULL KNOWLEDGE REGARDING THE DISPUTE. PASS THROUGH CLAIMS ARE PROPER TO PROVIDE A MEANS OF RECOVERY FOR LOWER TIER SUBCONTRACTORS WHO LACK A REMEDY, WHICH IS NOT THE CASE HERE. (SUPERIOR COURT. V. MITZEL (2004) 117 CAL.APP.4TH 301, 314. PROGRESS, AS NIBBI'S ASSIGNEE, CAN RECOVER BASED ON A THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY THEORY OF RECOVERY. AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THERE IS NO INCONSISTENCY OR LEGAL DOCTRINE THAT BARS PROGRESS FROM SUING IN ITS OWN RIGHT AS A PARTY TO A CONTRACT AND AS AN ASSIGNEE OF NIBBI TO VINDICATE NIBBI'S THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY RIGHTS. DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT WINCO WINDOW COMPANY INC.'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT OF PROGRESS GLASS COMPANY, INC. is overruled as to the second cause of action for fraud and deceit and the sixth cause of action for third party beneficiary breach of contract claim as the assignee of Nibbi and sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action for a pass through claim as an assigned of Nibbi. The second cause of action is alleged with sufficient particularity and the pleading requirements are relaxed where the cross-defendant ought to possess full knowledge regarding the dispute. Pass through claims are proper to provide a means of recovery for lower tier subcontractors who lack a remedy, which is not the case here. (Superior Court. v. Mitzel (2004) 117 Cal.App.4th 301, 314. Progress, as Nibbi's assignee, can recover based on a third party beneficiary theory of recovery. As to the sixth cause of action, there is no inconsistency or legal doctrine that bars Progress from suing in its own right as a party to a contract and as an assignee of Nibbi to vindicate Nibbi's third party beneficiary rights. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Irene L. Tofft, CSR #12913. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID F. MYERS OF THE WOLKIN CURRAN, LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-982-9390 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT;
ATTY. MARK ISSAC SCHICKMAN, TEL. NO. 1-415-541-0200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant/Cross-complainant Progress Glass Company's Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Cross-Complaint is GRANTED. " '[A]llegations of fraud involve a serious attack on character, and fairness to the defendant demands that he should receive the fullest possible details of the charge in order to prepare his defense.' " (Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 216.) Progress Glass' amended complaint alleges that Winco represented from March to June 2013 that it would ship glazing to Progress in June 2013. This representation appears to have been knowingly false. (Deposition of Rudy Selinger, p. 110.) Progress Glass relied on those misrepresentations and then incurred $500,000 in damages. Progress Glass' amended pleading, therefore, states a valid cause of action. Trial date is continued from Oct. 19 to January 18, 2016 at 9;30 a.m. in Dept. 206. Order re motion signed in open court. The Parties are to submit an order continuing the trial date. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489
